arts.ttl: Flick says Ter Stegen is ‘ready’ to return and offers Lewandowski update
arts.sum: As for Lewandowski, who is expected to miss the next three weeks with a hamstring injury, Flick also didn’t set a timetable for his return and say the club won’t rush Lewy’s comeback to ensure he’s fully healthy once he’s ready to play again. The coach revealed that Ter Stegen is finally ready to make his comeback after missing almost the entire season with a knee injury, but didn’t commit to a set date for the goalkeeper’s return.

arts.ttl: Hansi Flick responds to anger of several Barcelona stars during Celta Vigo clash
arts.sum: Barcelona head coach Hansi Flick has on Monday spoken out on the visible frustration displayed by a number of his players during the club’s most recent outing. Barcelona were of…

arts.ttl: Nike to Re-Release Barcelona 2000s Total 90 Kit
arts.sum: Nike is set to tap into nostalgia once again with the re-release of one of FC Barcelona’s most iconic kits from the early 2000s.

arts.ttl: Barcelona stars 2nd & 3rd: Most big chances created in Europe’s top-5 leagues this season
arts.sum: Two leading members of the attacking ranks find themselves among the podium places when it comes to the players with the most big chances created in Europe’s top-5 leagues this…

arts.ttl: Nico Paz on Como future, Real Madrid and surprising Yamal dream
arts.sum: Como talent Nico Paz admits he ‘really wants to continue’ in Serie A, but worries...

arts.ttl: FC Barcelona: Raphinha targeted by THREE Premier League clubs as €95m transfer battle erupts
arts.sum: The Premier League trio have been alerted to the possible availability of Barcelona winger Raphinha on the market this summer and, according to TBR Football, all three are considering launching a bid in the region of €93 million (£80m). Raphinha is said to be putting the feelers out in order to try to manufacture a potential summer move, with his representatives said to have contacted the Premier League teams to alert them to the potential availability of the 28-year-old.

arts.ttl: ‘I want to see the right reaction ‘ Flick sends message to frustrated Fati and Fort
arts.sum: Hansi Flick sent a message to Ansu Fati and Héctor Fort in a press conference on Monday after the two young players were caught having disappointed reactions to not being used as substitutes in the comeback win over Celta Vigo on Saturday. Ansu and Héctor’s reactions are understandable given their lack of minutes all season long, but at the end of the day Barça were losing a game at home in the middle of a very tight title race and Flick made the substitutions he thought gave his team the best chance to win.

arts.ttl: “I don’t understand the reaction” – Hansi Flick calls out Barcelona duo for attitude
arts.sum: Barcelona manager Hansi Flick was openly critical of youngsters Ansu Fati and Hector Fort for their reactions as the Blaugrana completed a comeback from two goals down against Celta Vigo on Saturday. While he understood their frustration, Flick was not happy with their lack of self-control at Montjuic. arts.ttl: Barcelona target confirms plan to seal summer free transfer
arts.sum: Jonathan Tah confirms his decision to leave Bayer Leverkusen on a free transfer amid interest from Barcelona.

arts.ttl: Lucy Bronze’s rousing speech gives Chelsea hope for Barcelona second leg
arts.sum: Chelsea trail WCL holders 4-1 after first leg of semi-final Sonia Bompastor: ‘We need to believe it’s possible’ Lucy Bronze delivered a rousing post-match speech on the pitch at the Estadi Johan Cruyff, urging her Chelsea teammates to believe they can overturn a 4-1 Champions League semi-final deficit against Barcelona at Stamford Bridge on Sunday. The Chelsea head coach, Sonia Bompastor, revealed the gist of Bronze’s motivational plea after the heavy loss to the defending European champions. Bronze, who has won the European club title five times – more than any other English footballer, male or female, and equalled only by Gareth Bale among British footballers – pointed and gesticulated passionately as she spoke to the rest of the Chelsea team shortly after the final whistle.
